narrator.name: Sarah
narrator.style: smooth, warm, slightly mischievous female narrator

jasper.name: Josh
jasper.style: energetic sunny young male, golden retriever energy

voices.text: Cat Top Sim. Suki's Jealous Reign.
Five boyfriends. Five spotless floors of evidence. One very smug cat.
Heather will buy more trinkets. Suki will be waiting.
Wow, Heather, your kitchen is so— wait. Is that cat… staring at me?
…Did something just explode in the kitchen?
That is the fourth mug. Should we… should we stop her? She looks so calm about it.
I can't look away. It's like a tiny, furry avalanche with eye contact.
Oh—hi, little one. You're much more interesting than the documentary, aren't you?
Was this whole thing… for attention? Honestly? Respect.
Movie night! I brought popcorn, I brought candy, I— oh my gosh, HI KITTY!
WHOA! Cat tornado! That was awesome— are you okay, buddy?!
She is SPEED-RUNNING your coffee table! Should I be filming this? I am filming this.
That candle had a family, man! …Do it again.
Haha, okay okay! Up you go, chaos queen. You're the MVP tonight!
You are SO much better than the movie. No offense to the movie.
I only work at night, when the light is honest. …Your cat is judging my posture.
That was… a statement piece. Abstract glass on hardwood.
Destruction as critique. She hates my sketchbook. She might be my best critic.
The laptop too? Brutal. Brave. Devastatingly curated.
Alright, diva. Come here. You're the only gallery worth visiting tonight.
I shall title this evening: Jealousy, with Fur. My masterpiece.
I made choux pastry from scratch! It only took nine hours— oh! Hello, your majesty.
The perfume! That was a gift! …Please not the jewelry box.
She looked me in the eye while she did that one. IN THE EYE, Heather.
I can't compete with this. She's magnificent. I bake for a living and I'm the side dish.
Okay, I understand now. It's your apartment. We're just baking in it.
You're softer than my brioche. Don't tell my brioche.
A table is an instrument. Tonight we dine in D minor. …The cat has already begun.
The first violin of glassware has fallen. Fascinating. Continue.
Four glasses, one candelabra. She composes in percussion.
I was told this would be a quiet dinner. I have never been so… moved.
Encore finished. Come here, maestro. You conducted the whole evening.
Is this… laughter? Ah. So that is what it feels like. Thank you, small tyrant.
